name: cover-title

name: cover-subtitle
text:
Behavior-first cyber resilience for
critical national infrastructure
textPreview: Behavior-first cyber resilience for | critical national infrastructure

name: cover-loop
text: DETECT   →   EXPLAIN   →   ANTICIPATE   →   CONTAIN

name: cover-proof
text:
A working FastAPI + React prototype with two evaluated AI engines,
an explainable ATT&CK graph, and human-gated response.
textPreview: A working FastAPI + React prototype with two evaluated AI engines, | an explainable ATT&CK graph, and human-gated response.

name: cover-date
text: Technical pitch deck  •  Verified 19 July 2026

alt: Critical infrastructure systems connected through a cyber-resilience layer that contains one anomalous path

text:
Open with the outcome: this is a live, evidence-traceable SOC decision layer, not a static dashboard.
The two engines detect behavior and predict/attribute attacker activity; a shared spine turns those outputs into a graph and gated response.
Visual: generated specifically for this deck; no factual content is encoded in the illustration.

This slide paraphrases the official PS7 challenge. The only short direct phrase is ‘weeks to hours.’
The five optional build areas in the brief are illustrative, not mandatory. We selected anomaly detection, APT prediction/attribution, response orchestration, and attack-path modeling.
Source: 6a38ce305640d_ET_AI_Hackathon_2026_Problem_Statements.pdf, pages 14-15.

preview: PS7 requirement | What we built | Verified evidence / boundary

text:
Use this as the judge traceability slide: every core verb in the challenge has a corresponding component and evidence.
Be explicit that live OT connectors and vulnerability prioritization are not shipped. The brief labels its five example build areas as illustrative only.
Live campaign verification: 2,732 events, 1,192 alerts, 104 accounts, 479 nodes, 502 edges.

name: novel-right-title-1

name: novel-right-body-1
text: Connects accounts and hosts into a path, exposes pivots, critical assets, and a measurable isolation cut.
textPreview: Connects accounts and hosts into a path, exposes pivots, critical assets, and a measurable isolation cut.

name: novel-left-title-2
text: ‘Deep’ model by default

name: novel-right-title-2
text: Simple model wins when evidence says so
textPreview: Simple model wins when evidence says so

name: novel-right-body-2
text: First-order Markov reaches 36.5% top-3; LSTM reaches 27.2%. We ship the stronger, explainable model.
textPreview: First-order Markov reaches 36.5% top-3; LSTM reaches 27.2%. We ship the stronger, explainable model.

name: novel-left-title-3
text: Auto-response on / off

name: novel-right-title-3
text: Blast-radius-aware human gate

name: novel-right-body-3
text: Response mode depends on severity and critical-asset exposure; every proposed action is retained for audit.
textPreview: Response mode depends on severity and critical-asset exposure; every proposed action is retained for audit.

name: novel-loop
text: Differentiator: DETECT → CORRELATE → MAP → PREDICT → ATTRIBUTE → CONTAIN — every step inspectable.
textPreview: Differentiator: DETECT → CORRELATE → MAP → PREDICT → ATTRIBUTE → CONTAIN — every step inspectable.

text:
Do not claim invention of IsolationForest, MiniLM, Markov chains, or NetworkX metrics. The novelty is the integrated, auditable decision loop.
The model-selection evidence is reproducible: Markov top-3 36.54%, LSTM 27.2%, kill-chain baseline 7.05%.
The response layer is deliberately gated and simulated, which is a safety feature for the prototype.

name: arch-product-title
text: FASTAPI  +  REACT SOC COMMAND CENTER

name: arch-product-body
text: CSV / scenario / SSE inputs  •  9 operational views + login  •  cached fallback  •  one-container deploy
textPreview: CSV / scenario / SSE inputs  •  9 operational views + login  •  cached fallback  •  one-container deploy

text:
Walk top to bottom. Datasets and threat knowledge feed two separate engines. Both engines feed one per-request spine.
The frontend has nine operational routes plus the login splash; older documentation says eight, but App.jsx and Sidebar.jsx show nine.
The runtime uses precomputed embeddings and slim dependencies; no Torch is required for inference.

name: e1-design-title
text: What the model sees

name: e1-design-body
text:
New destination / source for user • running fan-out
fail rate • destination rarity • NTLM indicator
Labels are used only after scoring for evaluation.
textPreview: New destination / source for user • running fan-out | fail rate • destination rarity • NTLM indicator | Labels are used only after scorin...

name: e1-honesty
text: HONEST LIMIT: strict low-FPR recall is modest; correlation and graph context amplify weak event-level signals.
textPreview: HONEST LIMIT: strict low-FPR recall is modest; correlation and graph context amplify weak event-level signals.

text:
CICIDS experiment: 2,094,130 processed flows; time split by day; train is benign-only; test includes unseen attack families.
The PR-AUC chart compares like-for-like metrics. Do not mix the LANL ROC-AUC values into this chart.
LANL official dataset source: https://csr.lanl.gov/data/cyber1/
Caveat: at 1% FPR, TPR is 51.4%; at 5% FPR it is 96.9%. The project therefore uses correlation as a second layer.

name: e2-attribute-title
text: Attribute: transparent retrieval

name: e2-attribute-body
text:
172 actor profiles
55% coverage + 20% Jaccard + 25% semantic
Returns matched techniques and a templated justification.
textPreview: 172 actor profiles | 55% coverage + 20% Jaccard + 25% semantic | Returns matched techniques and a templated justification.

name: e2-caveat-text
text: NON-CIRCULAR CHECK: 4 manual report-ordered sequences score 10.0% top-3 — small, hard, and kept visible.
textPreview: NON-CIRCULAR CHECK: 4 manual report-ordered sequences score 10.0% top-3 — small, hard, and kept visible.

text:
The reproducible baseline rerun gives Markov 36.54% top-3 and kill-chain 7.05%, a 5.18x lift. Use 36.5% and 5.2x.
The full sequence corpus has 622 techniques; the training vocabulary used for prediction has 566.
Attribution is retrieval over public ATT&CK group profiles, not a trained classifier. The 100% self-retrieval evaluation is intentionally not headlined as independent accuracy.
The four manual sequences contain only 30 prediction points and six OOV next techniques; 10% is a caveat, not a headline.

textPreview: tracked across four attacker pivots | with one consistent incident context

name: spine-stat-caption-2
text:
host nodes / movement edges

textPreview: host nodes / movement edges | backed by drill-down event evidence

text:
The pipeline runs once per request and returns one bundle consumed by every screen. The cached sample is generated through the same function.
SSE is a paced replay of already-computed real scores; it is not yet a true incremental streaming inference engine.
The campaign graph has 479 nodes and 502 unique host-pair edges. The current default critical assets are a documented heuristic, not LANL ground truth.

name: feas-industry
text: Context, not a claim: M-Trends 2025 reports an 11-day global median dwell time for 2024. The demo’s ‘immediate’ alert is measured only inside its sampled log.
textPreview: Context, not a claim: M-Trends 2025 reports an 11-day global median dwell time for 2024. The demo’s ‘immediate’ alert is measured only in...

text:
Verification used Miniconda scikit-learn 1.9.0 to load models serialized with 1.7.2, producing compatibility warnings; 29 tests still passed. Deployment requirements pin 1.7.2.
Frontend Vite build completed successfully.
The 463 figure is graph descendants of C17693, while 475 is total exposure across all four pivots. Do not say isolation cuts all 475.
Latest industry source used here: https://cloud.google.com/blog/topics/threat-intelligence/m-trends-2025/

name: road-close
text: The MVP already closes detect → explain → anticipate → contain; the roadmap adds production trust, data breadth, and real-world response.
textPreview: The MVP already closes detect → explain → anticipate → contain; the roadmap adds production trust, data breadth, and real-world response.

text:
Phase 1 is operational hardening, not new AI. Phase 2 directly covers the optional vulnerability-prioritization track and the brief’s IT/OT ambition.
Phase 3 introduces real action connectors only after approval, audit and monitoring foundations exist.
Phase 4 grows the current attack-path graph into a true cyber-resilience digital twin.

name: comp-footnote
text: * ‘Not highlighted’ is not proof of absence; custom rules, add-ons and new releases may change coverage. Resilience Graph AI is a prototype, while the compared products are enterprise platforms.
textPreview: * ‘Not highlighted’ is not proof of absence; custom rules, add-ons and new releases may change coverage. Resilience Graph AI is a prototy...

preview: Platform | Behavior + correlation | ATT&CK / graph | Explicit next move | Response | Best fit / difference

text:
Comparison reflects public vendor positioning reviewed 19 July 2026 and avoids claiming that a non-highlighted capability is absent.
Microsoft Sentinel UEBA: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/sentinel/identify-threats-with-entity-behavior-analytics
Microsoft Sentinel playbooks: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/sentinel/automation/create-playbooks
Splunk RBA: https://www.splunk.com/en_us/resources/risk-based-alerting-feature-brief.html
Splunk SOAR: https://www.splunk.com/en_us/products/splunk-security-orchestration-and-automation.html
Darktrace platform / AI Analyst: https://www.darktrace.com/platform and https://www.darktrace.com/cyber-ai-analyst
CrowdStrike Next-Gen SIEM: https://www.crowdstrike.com/en-us/platform/next-gen-siem/
Key message: do not claim greater maturity. Claim a transparent, locally verifiable, next-technique decision loop designed around the hackathon brief.
